mapped_concepts.concept_summary: A complete binary tree is a binary tree where all internal nodes have two children, and all leaves have the same depth.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   Complete binary tree
®   All internal nodes have two children
®   All leaves have depth d
±   Essentially complete binary tree
®   A complete binary tree down to a depth of d-1
®   The nodes with depth d are as far to the left as possible
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Complete Binary Tree는 내부 노드가 두 자식을 가지고 모든 리프가 동일한 깊이를 갖는 바이너리 트리로 정의되어 있다. 이 개념은 비교를 위한 여러 특징이 있어 선택형 문제에 적절하지만 활동이 복잡하지 않고 수치 데이터를 다루지 않기 때문에 계산형 문제에는 적합하지 않다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: A heap is a type of binary tree where each node's value is greater than or equal to the values of its children, maintaining a complete binary tree structure. By removing the root key and maintaining the heap property, keys are extracted in nonincreasing order.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±    Heap
®   An essentially complete binary tree such that
Heap      ®   The values stored at the nodes come from an ordered set
property   ®   The value stored at each node is greater than or equal to the
values stored at its children
®   If we repeatedly remove the key stored at the root while
maintaining the heap property, the keys will be removed in
nonincreasing order
®   The key at the root is replaced with the key stored at the bottom
node (the far-right leaf) è siftdown
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Heap은 구조적 특성과 동작 방식을 설명하는 개념이다. 다양한 상황에서의 성질과 과정을 설명하므로 서술형 문제에 적합하다. 개념이 복잡하여 단순 사실 확인형 문제에는 조금 덜 적합하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: The siftdown process involves restoring the heap property by moving a new root down the heap until its position satisfies the heap property.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   Remove key at root
±   Replace key at root with key stored at the bottom node (far right leaf) and
deleting bottom node (decrement heap size)
±   Sift new root down the heap until heap property restored
®   Compare key at root with larger of the keys of the root’s children
®   If key at root is smaller, exchange keys
±   Repeat process down the tree until the key at node is not smaller than the
larger of the children
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Siftdown Process는 힙의 성질을 복구하는 과정에 대한 설명으로, 메커니즘 설명이 필요하고 순차적인 과정 설명이 포함되기 때문에 서술형 문제에 적합하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: Computational complexity studies the efficiency of algorithms in solving a problem and establishes lower bounds for algorithm performance.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   Study of all possible algorithms that solve a given problem
±   Determine a lower bound on efficiency of all algorithms for a given
problem
±   Problem analysis as opposed to algorithm analysis
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Computational Complexity는 알고리즘의 효율성을 분석하는 복합적인 주제로, 알고리즘의 상하한선을 설정하는 등의 설명이 들어가 있어 서술형 문제가 가장 적합하다. 단순 나열이 아니라 논리적 설명이 필요하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: Sorting is the process of arranging records according to a key field, where comparison-based algorithms can determine the order by comparing keys.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   Re-arrange records according to a key field
±   Algorithms that sort by comparison of keys can compare 2 keys to
determine which is larger and can copy keys
±   Cannot do other operations on them
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Sorting은 여러 알고리즘과 비교될 수 있는 주제로, 선택형 문제에 적합하다. 다만, 단순 정렬의 개념만 설명되어 있어 복잡한 설명을 요구하지 않는다.

mapped_concepts.concept_name: Worst-case Time Complexity of Insertion Sort
mapped_concepts.concept_summary: The worst-case time complexity of the insertion sort algorithm occurs when the keys are initially in non-increasing order, leading to at most θ(n^2) comparisons.
mapped_concepts.source_title: Worst-case Time Complexity Analysis of Number
mapped_concepts.source_context:
of Comparisons of Keys
±   Basic Operation: Comparison of S[j] with x
±   Input size: n, the number of keys to be sorted
±   Prior to loop execution, j set to i-1
®   j decremented at each loop iteration
®   j > 0 clause of the while expression becomes FALSE after i-1
iterations of the while loop
±   While loop executes from i = 2 to n
®   Total number of comparisons is at most
= θ(!2)
±   Worst-case behavior of Insertion sort
®   If the keys are originally in non-increasing order in the array
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Insertion Sort의 최악 시간 복잡도는 수치적 계산과 관련된 개념으로, 구체적인 데이터와 예시를 통해 계산 형식의 문제가 가능하다. 따라서 계산형 문제에 적합하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: Inversions in a permutation are pairs of elements where the earlier element is greater than the latter in the sequence.
mapped_concepts.source_title: Lower Bounds Sort by Comparison of Keys
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   Sorting n distinct keys that come from any ordered set
®    Assume that the keys to be sorted are simply the positive
integers
±   Permutation and Inversion
±   Example: [3, 2, 4, 1, 6, 5]
® Inversions: (3,2), (3,1), (2,1), (6,5), (4,1)
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Inversion in Permutation은 순열의 역전 개수를 다루며 이는 계산에 기반한 문제로, 계산형 문제에 적합하다. 구체적 예시가 주어질 수 있는 문제 유형이다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: The permutation [n, n-1, ..., 1] has the maximum number of inversions, n(n-1)/2, requiring n(n-1)/2 comparisons to sort in the worst case.
mapped_concepts.source_title: Proof of Theorem 7.1 in the worst case
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   Show there is a permutation with n(n-1)/2 inversions
±   At most one inversion is removed with each comparison è n(n-1)/2
comparisons
±   Show [n, n-1, n-2, . . . , 3, 2, 1] is such a permutation
®   n-1 inversions with n
®   n-2 inversions with n-1
…
®   1 inversion with 2
è (n-1) + (n-2) + … + 1 = n(n-1)/2 inversions
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Worst Case Inversions in Sorting은 최악의 경우에서의 계산을 다루고 있어, 특정 수치나 공식을 계산하는 문제에 적합하다. 따라서 계산형 문제가 적절하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: Merge Sort is a divide-and-conquer algorithm that divides the input array into smaller subarrays, sorts them, and then merges them back together, removing inversions and using extra space.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   Input in reverse order: S = [4,3,2,1]
±   3 and 1 are compared and 1 placed in output array
®   Inversions (3,1) and (4,1) removed
±   3 and 2 are compared and 2 placed in output array
®   Inversions (3,2) and (4,2) removed
±   Extra space usage is θ(n)
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Merge Sort Algorithm은 분할 정복을 통한 과정 설명으로, 여러 과정이 맞물려 매커니즘을 설명해야 하므로 서술형 문제에 적합하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_name: Time Complexity of Merge Sort Assignments
mapped_concepts.concept_summary: The slide explains the time complexity of assignments in the Merge sort algorithm, specifically stating that both comparisons followed by record assignments and copy operations each contribute n∙lg n assignments, resulting in 2n∙lg n.
mapped_concepts.source_title: Every-case time complexity of the number of
mapped_concepts.source_context:
assignments of records done by Merge sort
±   Every comparison in Merge is followed by a record assignment, so
we have » n∙lg n assignments in the Merge part of the algorithm
±   Add the copy operations, which are also » n∙lg n, because the
division tree is lg n levels deep
è 2n∙lg n
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Merge Sort Assignments의 시간 복잡도는 특정 숫자 계산을 다루고 있어 계산형 문제가 적합하다. 구체적인 수식을 통해 설명할 수 있다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: The time complexity of Merge Sort in all cases (best, average, and worst) is O(n log n) due to the divide and conquer method it utilizes to sort elements.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   The every-case time complexity of the number of assignments of
records è n∙lg n
Sorting
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Merge Sort의 시간 복잡도는 함수 및 계산식으로 표현되기 때문에 계산형 문제로 적합하다. 시간 복잡도와 관련된 수식을 통해 설명할 수 있다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: Interpolation Search is a search algorithm that assumes keys are evenly distributed and checks a position based on a calculated guess rather than the middle element, offering an average-case time complexity of log(log n), which is better than binary search.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   We can use other information in our search
®   e.g. Assume that the keys are close to being evenly distributed
between the first one and the last one.
®   Instead of checking whether x equals the middle key, we can
check whether x equals the key that is located about where we
would expect to find x è Interpolation Search
More Computational Complexity:                    5
The Searching Problems
§ The average-case time complexity = log(log 𝑛)
§ Better than Binary Search
§ The worst-case is similar to a sequential search
More Computational Complexity:                     6
The Searching Problems
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Interpolation Search는 검색 알고리즘으로 작동 원리와 복잡도의 비교적 설명을 필요로 한다. 복잡한 메커니즘을 설명해야 하므로 서술형 문제에 적합하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: Interpolation search is a search algorithm that has an average-case time complexity of Θ(log(log n)) under the assumption that keys are uniformly distributed, but its worst-case time complexity is Θ(log n), which is worse than that of binary search.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   The worst case happens when mid sets to low repeatedly
±   Under the assumptions that the keys are uniformly distributed,
®   The average-case time complexity is Θ(log(log 𝑛))
®   The worst-case time complexity is Θ( log 𝑛)! (still worse than
Binary Search)
More Computational Complexity:                   7
The Searching Problems
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Robust Interpolation Search Complexity는 이론적 시간 복잡도를 비교·분석하는 개념으로, 다양한 경우의 수를 설명하면서 비교할 수 있다. 이는 서술형 문제에 적합하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: A 3-2 tree is a type of B-tree where each node contains one or two keys; if a node contains one key, it has two children, and if it contains two keys, it has three children, and all leaves are at the same level.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   A 3-2 tree (the simplest version of B-trees) meets the following prop
erties
®   Each node contains one or two keys
®   If a nonleaf contains one key, it has two children, whereas if it
contains two keys, it has three children
®   The keys in the left subtree of a given node are less than or
equal to the key stored at that node
®   The keys in the right subtree of a given node are greater than
or equal to the key stored at that node
®   If a node contains two keys, the keys in the middle subtree of
the node are greater than or equal to the left key and less than
or equal to the right key
®   All leaves are at the same level
More Computational Complexity:                      8
The Searching Problems
More Computational Complexity:   9
The Searching Problems
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: 3-2 Tree Properties는 다양한 트리의 특성을 보여주며, 이들을 비교하여 설명할 수 있어 선택형 문제에 적합하다. 주어진 조건들을 나열하고 비교할 수 있다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: The Traveling Salesperson Problem is a classic NP-Complete problem that involves finding the shortest possible route visiting a set of points and returning to the origin point. No algorithm has been found with better than exponential time complexity in the worst case.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   No algorithm has ever been developed with a worst-case time
complexity better than exponential
±   It has never been proven that such an algorithm is not possible
±   NP-Complete
NP-Complete Problems
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: TSP는 인과관계 및 복잡성 설명이 필요한 NP-Complete 문제로, 복잡성과 효율성에 대해 서술형 설명 요구가 요구된다. 이는 비교적 분석형 문제로 사용될 수 있다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: Intractability refers to problems that are difficult for computers to solve efficiently, often lacking identified efficient algorithms, and frequently involving exponential algorithms which are impractical for larger inputs.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   Dictionary Definition of intractable: “difficult to treat or work.”
±   Computer Science: problem is intractable if a computer has
difficulty solving it
®   No efficient algorithms have been identified
®   Not proved that the problem requires a lot of time
Æ   Many optimization problems are of this nature
±   Exponential algorithms are useless except for very small inputs
NP-Complete Problems
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Intractability in Computer Science는 여러 문제의 성질을 설명하는 복합적인 주제를 포함하고 있어 다양한 인과관계 설명이 필요해 서술형 문제에 적합하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: A problem is considered tractable if there exists an algorithm that can solve it with a polynomial-bound time complexity as a function of its input size.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   A problem is tractable if there exists a polynomial-bound algorithm
that solves it.
±   Worst-case growth rate can be bounded by a polynomial
±   Function of its input size
±   ! " = $% "& + ⋯ + $) ") + $* where k is a constant
±   ! " ∈ , "&
±   "log " not a polynomial
®   "log " < "1 bound by a polynomial
NP-Complete Problems
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Tractable Problems는 컴퓨터 과학의 문제 해결에 대한 복잡성과 효율성을 다루고 있어 인과관계 설명이 필요하여 서술형 문제에 적합하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: In computer science, a problem is considered intractable if it cannot be solved by an algorithm with a polynomial time complexity, making it difficult or impossible to compute efficiently.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   “Difficult to treat or work”
±   A problem in Computer Science is intractable if a computer has
difficulty solving it
±   A problem is intractable if it is not tractable
±   Any algorithm with a growth rate not bounded by a polynomial
±   !", ! .%&' , "()* ' , "!,
±   Property of the problem (not the algorithm)
NP-Complete Problems
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Intractable Problem은 문제의 용이성에 대한 이론적 설명을 필요로 하는 복잡한 주제를 소개하므로 서술형 문제에 적합하다. 컴퓨터 과학에서의 이론적 설명이 필요하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: Computational problems are categorized into three groups: those with polynomial-time algorithms, those proven to be intractable, and those without proven intractability but lack polynomial-time solutions.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
1.   Problems for which polynomial-time algorithms have been found
2.   Problems that have been proven to be intractable
3.   Problems that have not been proven to be intractable, but for which
polynomial-time algorithms have never been found
®   e.g. 0-1 Knapsack problem, Traveling Salesperson problem,
and the Sum-of-Subsets problem
NP-Complete Problems
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Categories of Computational Problems는 다양한 문제 종류를 나열하고 설명할 수 있는 성격을 가져, 복잡성이나 트랙터블/인트랙터블과 같은 여러 카테고리를 비교 분석하는 서술형 또는 선택형 문제에 적합하다.

mapped_concepts.concept_summary: Polynomial-time algorithms are those whose time complexity is represented by a polynomial expression such as O(n), O(n log n), or O(n^k). They are tractable and efficient for many computational problems.
mapped_concepts.source_context:
±   ! " log " for sorting
±   ! log " for searching
±   ! "& for Floyd’s algorithm for shortest paths
±   Algorithms with the following worst-case time complexities are all
polynomial time
®   2", 3"& + 4", 5" + "-. , "log "
NP-Complete Problems
mapped_concepts.mapping_reason: Polynomial-time Algorithms는 여러 알고리즘의 시간 복잡도를 다루고 있어 다양한 시간 복잡도의 종류를 비교하여 설명할 수 있다. 따라서 선택형 또는 서술형 문제에 적합하다.
